title: Ottawa Investment Board - May 22, 2026
meeting_location: Colonel By Room, 110 Laurier Avenue West

agenda_items.title: Notices and meeting information for meeting participants and the public
agenda_items.description:
Notices and meeting information are attached to the agenda and minutes, including: availability of simultaneous interpretation and accessibility accommodations; in camera meeting procedures; information items not subject to discussion; personal information disclaimer for correspondents and public speakers; notices regarding minutes; and in person participation details.
Accessible formats and communication supports are available, upon request.The deadline to register to speak, or submit written comments or visual presentations is 9:30 am on May 20, 2026 (48 hours before the start of the meeting).
agenda_items.minutes: These “Summary Minutes” indicate the disposition of items and actions taken at the meeting. This document does not include all of the text that will be included in the full Minutes, such as the record of written and oral submissions. Recorded votes and dissents contained in the Summary Minutes are draft until the full Minutes of the meeting are confirmed by the Board. The draft of the full Minutes (for confirmation) will be published with the agenda for the next regular Board meeting and, once confirmed, will replace this document.

agenda_items.title: Declarations of Interest
agenda_items.minutes: No Declarations of Interest were filed.

sub_agendas_items.title: 5.1 Investment Performance Review: Q1 2026
sub_agendas_items.description:
A presentation for Members will be provided by representatives from the Board’s Outsourced Chief Investment Officer (OCIO).
If questions relate to information that is proprietary to the OCIO, this session may close the meeting to the public, pursuant to the Board’s Procedure By-law, Subsection 24(i) permitting closed meetings when considering “A trade secret or scientific, technical, commercial, financial or labour relations information, supplied in confidence to the Board, which, if disclosed, could reasonably be expected to prejudice significantly the competitive position or interfere significantly with the contractual or other negotiations of a person, group of persons, or organization.”

motions.motion_text: BE IT RESOLVED THAT the Ottawa Investment Board resolve to move in camera pursuant to Subsection 24(i) of the Ottawa Investment Board Procedure By-law, as the subject matter relates to: “A trade secret or scientific, technical, commercial, financial or labour relations information, supplied in confidence to the Board, which, if disclosed, could reasonably be expected to prejudice significantly the competitive position or interfere significantly with the contractual or other negotiations of a person, group of persons, or organization” and will not be reported out.
